commands.author: ZOOWH
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an `interactive-debug` provider recommendation use case with aliases, scoring logic, Go tests, command docs, provider landscape notes, and changelog text.

Reproducibility: no. user bug reproduction is applicable because this is a feature PR. The scoring concern is source-reproducible from the new `hasTarget(targetWindows)` check and the existing Windows target formatting behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can update the interactive-debug target matching and regression coverage; maintainers still own the product decision to land the feature.

target.title: fix(telegram): materialize block-boundary newlines in rich HTML for Bot API 10.1
target.body:
## Summary Bot API 10.1 rich messages treat literal newlines between block-level elements as insignificant whitespace, collapsing them instead of rendering line breaks. When the `/status` card (or any multi-line text built with single `\n` separators) is routed through the rich-message path, every field merges into a single run-on line. The root cause is that `convertTelegramRichSegmentNewlines` skips pure-newline segments that hug a structural tag (prev/next block), assuming the block tag itself produces a visual break. This assumption holds in browsers but not in Bot API 10.1 rich messages. Fix: when a segment between two block tags is purely whitespace newlines (no text content) and not inside a rich container (table/figure/details/ul/ol), materialize the newlines as `
` so block boundaries remain visible. Also adds `ul` and `ol` to the rich container tracking set (`TELEGRAM_RICH_LINE_BREAK_CONTAINER_TAGS`) so that pretty-printed list HTML (e.g. `
    \n
  • …
    • \n
`) does not get spurious `
` children injected inside the list container. Fixes #95538 ## Root Cause In `extensions/telegram/src/format.ts`, `convertTelegramRichSegmentNewlines` has `hugsPrev`/`hugsNext` logic that keeps bare `\n` runs literal when they hug a structural tag. This is correct for browsers (block tags produce their own line break) but incorrect for Bot API 10.1 rich messages, where bare newlines between block elements collapse as insignificant whitespace. Additionally, the container tracking set (`TELEGRAM_RICH_LINE_BREAK_CONTAINER_TAGS`) omitted `ul` and `ol`, meaning pretty-printed list HTML could receive unwanted `
` injections inside list containers. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Telegram `/status` card delivered as a single run-on line when `richMessages: true`. Block-boundary newlines between fields collapse. **Environment tested:** Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux, OpenClaw main branch and PR branch. **Before fix (upstream/main — bug present):** ``` materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks("

Plan

\n

Details

") → "

Plan

\n

Details

" ← bare \n preserved, collapses in rich messages ``` **After fix (PR branch — bug fixed):** ``` materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks("

Plan

\n

Details

") → "

Plan


Details

" ← \n materialized as
, visible in rich messages ``` **Container protection (ul/ol — no spurious
):** ``` materializeTelegramRichHtmlLineBreaks("
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
") → "
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
" ← newlines preserved inside list container ``` **/status card simulation:** ``` Input: "

Status

\n

Uptime: 39s

\n

Model: claude

" Output: "

Status


Uptime: 39s


Model: claude

" → Each field on its own line ✅ ``` **Pretty-printed list protection:** ``` Input: "
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
" Output: "
    \n
  • One
    • \n
  • Two
    • \n
" → No spurious
inside list ✅ ``` **Supersedes:** #95732 (closed — same fix with added ul/ol container protection per ClawSweeper review) ## Checklist - [x] I have read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [x] My changes are covered by tests - [x] I have tested my changes locally
